---
title: "winsorize"
generated: true
description: "Clip `image` to its `[lower, upper]` intensity percentiles."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.metrics.preprocess`](../nitrix-metrics-preprocess.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:winsorize -->
```python
winsorize(image: Float[Array, '...'], *, lower: float = 0.005, upper: float = 0.995) -> Float[Array, '...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:winsorize -->
Clip `image` to its `[lower, upper]` intensity percentiles.

The fMRIPrep `--winsorize-image-intensities [0.005, 0.995]` step: the
bottom / top tails are clamped to the percentile values, so a handful of
extreme voxels cannot dominate the intensity-based cost.  Pure and
differentiable.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:winsorize -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `image` | `Float` | `...` | — | Any-shape intensity array (the percentiles are over **all** elements). |
| `lower` | `float` | — | `0.005` | Lower / upper clip percentiles in `[0, 1]` (default `0.005` / `0.995`, the ANTs / fMRIPrep convention). |
| `upper` | `float` | — | `0.995` | Lower / upper clip percentiles in `[0, 1]` (default `0.005` / `0.995`, the ANTs / fMRIPrep convention). |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...`. The intensity array with the same shape as `image`, its values clamped to lie within the `lower` and `upper` intensity percentiles.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:winsorize -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
